svg.text: yata — Rust Cypher Graph Engine Architecture
12 crates | 854 tests | CSR + ArrowFragment + R2 | Shannon Information-Theoretic Analysis
deps: grin, cypher, vineyard, core, memmap2
deps: engine, graph, s3, object, axum, tokio, metrics
App → PDS_RPC.createRecord → Pipeline.send() (durable, ~1ms) → YATA_RPC.mergeRecord() (CSR append, no page-in) → dirty=true
Snapshot: cron 1min, dirty-gated → csr_to_fragment() → R2 PUT (full compaction, dirty labels only [DESIGN])
O(N) serialize even for 1 dirty label
P3: QueryCache + CSR + ArrowFragment = triple memory residence
Shannon redundancy ~200% — same data in 3 representations. LRU eviction is the only relief valve.
P4: Full Page-In on Cold Start
ALL labels from R2 = O(N) network
P6: Pipeline WAL + R2 Snapshot = dual persistence (Shannon redundancy ~100%)
Both Pipeline WAL (R2 JSON, 10s) and snapshot (R2 ArrowFragment) persist the same data. Snapshot = pure checkpoint.
Re-fetch from R2 on eviction → network amplification
500M edge claim but page faults are OS-dependent
Single vCPU bottleneck, write lock duration
Blocks snapshot cron on single vCPU
Shannon Efficiency Summary (H = entropy bits transferred / total bits moved)
Full page-in + full snapshot PUT = O(N) both ways
CSR rebuild O(V+E) per mutation, 1 vCPU
12 crates | ~22,000 LOC | 854 tests | Edition 2024 | Apache-2.0 | Cross-compile: cargo zigbuild x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
